title: Couple regression blocking to constraint severity—block only on blocking-constraint regressions
context: Regression Guard must prevent re-introducing deliberately-retired code without excessive gatekeeping. Hunch already ranks constraints by severity; regression enforcement should inherit that model to avoid indiscriminate blocking. Touches invariant(s): con_902759b3dc (All JSON writes to .hunch/ must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so an interrupted write can never truncate the index); con_a87360128b (Semantic vectors are a derived layer in SQLite, never the source of truth; reconcile by content hash on reindex).
decision: A regression only fails commits when the decision that retired the code is linked via source_decision to an active blocking constraint. All regressions warn; only blocking-linked ones fail strict commits. Regressions scoped to touched files; only in-force decisions match (superseded ignored).
consequences: Regression severity automatically tiers with constraint importance—blocking constraints trigger blocking regressions
Developers get both early advisory (edit-time hook) and commit-time enforcement without flow interruption
Maintains single source of truth: constraint severity model drives both invariant and regression gating
Requires decisions to link to constraints (source_decision) to participate in strict-mode regression checks
alternatives_rejected: Block all regressions in strict mode—too rigid; would gate on advisory-level removed code
Separate regression severity scoring—duplicates classification burden; reuse existing constraint model
Global regression matching—false positives; scope to diff-touched files for precision
Allow superseded decisions to block—anachronistic; gates against outdated design, not current policy
